english: within, out of; k'oṅ-du (khong du) (also koṅs-su (kongs su)) c'úd-pa (chud pa) or tsúd-pa (tsud pa), with or without sems (sems) (resp. t'ugs (thugs)) being prefixed 1. impressed on, fixed in the mind, thoroughly understood, known. 2. very restless, uneasy, sorry, anxious in one's mind; - k'oṅ-du sdú-ba (khong du sdu ba) to impress on the memory, to learn (by heart) Glr.; k'oṅ-nas snyiṅ jyúṅ-ba (khong nas snying byung ba) ltar as if their heart was torn out, Pth.; snyiṅ koṅ rús-pai (snying khong rus pai) dkyil-nas ysól-ba btab (dkyil nas gsol ba btab) he prayed from his inmost heart Thgy.; k'oṅ-nas śés-pa (khong nas shes pa), smrá-ba (smra ba) to know by heart, to say, recite by heart Cs. *K'óg-la yid-du meď* (*khog la yid du med*) W. I have no recollection of it; k'oṅ-pai dród-la p'an (khong pai drod la phan) it helps against internal heat Med.; k'oṅ-par soṅ-bai dug bziṅ-no (khong par song bai dug bzhin no) it is like a poison that has entered into the internal parts (or the veins) Thgy.; *k'óg-paṅk'an-pa* (*khog pang khan pa*), a bad character W., *k'óg-pa c'eṅ-mo* (*khog pa chen mo*) W. generosity, magnanimity (?) - Comp. koṅ-krág (khong khrag), the blood contained in the veins Cs. - Koṅ-Kro (khong khro) (-ba) wrath, anger; k'oṅ-kro spoṅ-ba (khong khro spong ba) Mil. to put away, subdue anger, *zá-ba* (*za ba*) C. to 'conceive' anger, take a dislike; koṅ-mi-kro-ba (khong mi khro ba) quiet, calm, mild Pth. - *kog-t'úg* (*khog thug*) col. uneasiness, sorrow, anxiety; *kog-t'úg jhé-pa* (*khog thug byed pa*) C., *cò-ce* (*co ce*) W. to be uneasy, anxious. - koṅ-gáṅ (khong gang) full, filled up in the inside, solid, koṅ-stóṅ (khong stong) hollow, tubular. - *kog-t'éṅ* (*khog theng*), *kog-ḍéṅ* (*khog dreng*), W. grudge, ill-will, hatred. - koṅ-tsil (khong tsil) suet. - *koṅ-lóg* (*khong log*) W. cholera. - *kog-śiṅ* (*khog shing*) W. 1. the core of a tree, heart-wood. 2. tenon. - *kog-śúgs* (*khog shugs*) a groan, sigh W., *kog-śúgs taṅ-ce* (*khog shugs tang ce*) to sigh, to groan. - *kog-śúbs-la śil-ce* (*khog shubs la shil ce*) W. to read low, softly, whisperingly; *kog-śil taṅ-ce* (*khog shil tang ce*) W. to read noiselessly, so as not to be heard. - koṅ-(r)seṅ (khong (r)seng) inner caverns, not opening to the daylight; (those of the Rirab are the habitations of the Lha-ma-yiṅ (lha ma yin) or Asura).

1. sbst. (koṅs-ma (khongs ma) Cs.?) the middle, the midst; gaṅs-koṅs-na (gangs khongs na) in the midst of alpine snows Mil.; respecting time: żág bdún-gyi koṅs-su (zhag bdun gyi khongs su) within, during, seven days Pth., Tar.; respecting money: de nyíd-kyi koṅs-na yód-so (de nyid kyi khongs na yod so), (this) is contained, included in that (sum) Tar. 32, 15; koṅs-su ytógs-pa (khongs su gtogs pa) Lex., Cs.: annexed to, united, incorporated with. - 2. adj. crooked; W.: *koṅs ča dug* (*khongs ca dug*) it is bent, curved, e.g. paper by heat, the limbs by the gout; *koṅs-kan* (*khongs kan*) W., *koṅ-ril* (*khong ril*) C. crippled.
I. v. k'ód-pa (khod pa) and gód-pa (god pa). - II. inst. of k'od (khod).

anger, grudge, resentment; k'on dzin-pa (khon dzin pa), k'on-du dzin-pa (khon du dzin pa) to hate, *k'on-la kúr-ce* (*khon la khur ce*) W. id.; *k'on-gúg-ste dúd-ce* (*khon gug ste dud ce*) W. ("to sit waiting with hatred") id.; *k'on-bér* (*khon ber*) W., the sting, the burning of anger or hatred in the soul.
1. fat, heavy, clumsy Sch. - 2. sometimes for k'og (khog). - 3. v. pebs-pa (phebs pa).

wallet, leather trunk C., Cs.: felt or skin bag; yzims-kóm (gzims khom) Cs. id. (prob. resp.); kom-bóg (khom bog) Cs. a cloak-bag; more accurately: the cloth in which the trunk is wrapped and carried by the porter.

Schr.; to be able, esp. to be enabled to do a thing by the absence of external impediments; k'óm-pa min (khom pa min) Cs., *k'óm-ce mi rag* (*khom ce mi rag*) W. I have no time, I cannot do it now; sdod mi k'om (sdod mi khom) I cannot sit and wait now Pth.; míd mi k'óm-par (mid mi khom par) without your having time to swallow it down Dzl. 7V°, 17. mi-k'óm-pa brgyad (mi khom pa brgyad), the eight obstacles to happiness, caused by the re-birth in places or situations unfavourable to conversion Trig. no. 66. Acc. to Schr. the word is also used in that special sense: to be able to carry on a law-suit, to which there are likewise eight obstacles.
Sch., k'or-yúg (khor yug), k'ór-sa (khor sa) v. k'o-ra (kho ra); k'or-yúg-tu (khor yug tu) continually, incessantly Mil.
Cs. = k'ól-bu (khol bu); k'ól-du jyúṅ-ba (khol du byung ba), abridgment, epitome Cs.
1. Cs. boiled. - 2. Sch. boiling, bubbling, zaṅs k'ól-pa (zangs khol pa) a bubbling kettle Dzl.
also k'ol-brán (khol bran), servant, man-servant, k'ól-por rjés-su bzúṅ-ba (khol por rjes su bzung ba) to
